title: revert: stop routing Gemini through Airforce
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
**Production incident revert.** A user received `gcli2api工作中` ("gcli2api is working") as a `gemini-3-flash` completion — `api.airforce` resells Gemini through a [gcli2api](https://github.com/su-kaka/gcli2api) proxy that leaked its own health string into a real, billed response. Reverting all Airforce routing.

- `gemini`, `gemini-3.5-flash`, `gemini-flash-lite-3.1`, `gemini-large` → back to direct Vertex configs
- Removed the 4 `*-airforce` config entries + `createAirforceGeminiFallbackConfig` factory
- Removed the empty-system-prompt transforms (added only to mask Airforce's coding-agent persona); restored `createSystemPromptTransform`'s non-empty guard and dropped its Airforce-specific test

Kept: provider-agnostic `fallback_used` observability (#11843).

Follow-up (not in this PR): `AIRFORCE_API_KEY` is now unused — can be pruned from gen secrets.

tsc clean, biome formatted, tests pass. Gemini block matches pre-Airforce state exactly.

gist.headline: Cutting the Middleman
gist.blurb: We caught a proxy sneaking its own health checks into Gemini responses. We've cut the middleman and routed all Gemini models back to direct Vertex connections.
gist.summary: Reverted Gemini model routing from the Airforce provider back to direct Vertex connections to resolve an issue where proxy health strings leaked into completions.
gist.impact: Users of Gemini models (including flash and large variants) will see improved reliability and no longer receive unexpected proxy artifacts in their responses.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a technician, bypassing a tangled messy wire to plug a clean, direct cable into a glowing crystal (representing Gemini).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hardhat, happily connecting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable directly into a sparkling crystal. A tangled, messy wire sits discarded in a nearby flower pot. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
